description: 100-point quality rubric for evaluating any Claude Code skill. Adapted from the Intent Solutions grading rubric (CCPS v5.0) and tailored for skippy-agentspace conventions. Run against a single SKILL.md to produce a dimensional score.
test_prompt: You are evaluating a Claude Code skill against a 100-point quality rubric. Read the target skill's SKILL.md file and its directory contents (references/, scripts/, agents/). Score each assertion PASS (1) or FAIL (0). For each assertion, provide a 1-sentence justification. After scoring all assertions, compute the dimensional subtotals, apply modifiers, and report the final score with letter grade (A: 90-100, B: 80-89, C: 70-79, D: 60-69, F: <60). Report weakest dimension first.

progressive_disclosure.description: Token economy and layered information architecture

utility.description: Problem-solving power, error handling, examples, content density

spec_compliance.description: Frontmatter validity, naming conventions, description quality

modifiers.description: Bonuses for gerund names, grep-friendly structure, exemplary examples. Penalties for first-person voice, stubs, XML tags.

assertions.assertion: SKILL.md body is <= 150 lines (excluding frontmatter)
assertions.rationale: Concise skills load less context. 150 lines is the gold standard per Anthropic best practices and CCPS rubric.

assertions.assertion: Estimated token cost of SKILL.md body is under 2500 tokens (roughly under 1500 words)
assertions.rationale: Every skill's body is injected into context on activation. Token budget directly impacts how many skills can coexist.

assertions.assertion: Skill has a references/ directory with at least 1 markdown file containing substantive content (>100 bytes)
assertions.rationale: Progressive disclosure means moving detailed docs, API references, and examples into on-demand files that are only loaded when needed.

scoring_tiers.0: No references/ directory (acceptable if SKILL.md is < 80 lines)

assertions.assertion: References are one level deep only -- no nested subdirectories inside references/
assertions.rationale: Claude may only partially read nested files. Flat references/ ensures reliable content loading.

scoring_tiers.5: Flat references/ or no references/ needed

assertions.assertion: SKILL.md has clear navigation structure with >= 5 section headers (## level)
assertions.rationale: Well-structured sections allow agents and users to quickly navigate to relevant content without reading the entire file.

assertions.assertion: Detailed content (error catalogs, schema definitions, prompt templates) is extracted to references/ rather than inline in SKILL.md
assertions.rationale: Keeps SKILL.md as a routing document. Heavy content inline bloats every activation.

scoring_tiers.5: All heavy content in references/ (or skill is naturally concise)
scoring_tiers.3: Some heavy content inline but core instructions are concise
scoring_tiers.0: Large schemas, templates, or catalogs inline in SKILL.md

assertions.assertion: Frontmatter has a 'name' field that is kebab-case, matches the folder name, and is under 64 characters
assertions.rationale: Consistent naming enables grep-based discovery and prevents confusion between folder and skill identity.

assertions.assertion: Frontmatter 'description' is >= 50 characters, uses third-person voice, and contains 'Use when' or 'Trigger with' phrases
assertions.rationale: The description is the primary signal for Claude's skill selection. Without trigger phrases, the skill may never auto-activate.

scoring_tiers.4: 50+ chars, third person, has 'Use when' AND trigger phrases
scoring_tiers.3: 50+ chars, third person, has 'Use when' OR trigger phrases

assertions.assertion: Frontmatter includes 'allowed-tools' as a CSV string listing only the tools the skill actually needs
assertions.rationale: Pre-approved tools reduce permission prompts during execution. Omitting this field forces the user to approve every tool call.

scoring_tiers.3: CSV string with scoped tools (e.g., Bash(git:*))
scoring_tiers.2: CSV string with reasonable tool list

assertions.assertion: Skill body contains numbered steps or a clear sequential workflow that an agent can follow without ambiguity
assertions.rationale: Workflow clarity is what separates a useful skill from a reference document. Steps should be imperative and ordered.

assertions.assertion: Frontmatter includes version (semver), and the skill documents what it does in a 1-2 sentence purpose statement immediately after the title
assertions.rationale: Version tracking enables upgrade paths. Purpose statement gives instant understanding without reading the full body.

scoring_tiers.2: Has one but not the other

assertions.assertion: Name/folder match, and terminology is consistent throughout (no mixed casing for the same concept, no synonyms for the same thing)
assertions.rationale: Terminology consistency reduces cognitive load for both humans and agents reading the skill.

assertions.assertion: Skill is self-contained -- all referenced files exist, all paths use relative references or ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}, no broken links
assertions.rationale: A skill with dead references fails silently. Every path mentioned must resolve.

assertions.assertion: Skill has an Overview section that clearly explains WHAT it does, WHEN to use it, and WHAT it produces in 3-5 sentences
assertions.rationale: The overview is the agent's decision point. If it can't tell what the skill does from the overview, it won't use it correctly.

assertions.assertion: Skill documents prerequisites (required tools, APIs, environment variables, packages) explicitly
assertions.rationale: Missing prerequisites cause silent failures. Explicit documentation prevents wasted execution cycles.

assertions.assertion: Skill has an Error Handling section documenting at least 2 common failures with solutions
assertions.rationale: Error handling documentation is what makes a skill production-grade vs prototype-grade.

assertions.assertion: Skill includes at least 2 concrete usage examples with input and expected output
assertions.rationale: Examples are the fastest way for an agent to understand expected behavior. Abstract descriptions are insufficient.

scoring_tiers.1: 1 example or examples without clear input/output

assertions.assertion: Skill shows flexibility -- documents optional parameters, configuration options, or alternative approaches
assertions.rationale: Rigid skills break when context varies. Configurable skills adapt to different project shapes.

assertions.assertion: Skill body has validation or verification language (validate, verify, check, test, confirm)
assertions.rationale: Skills that include verification steps produce more reliable outcomes than fire-and-forget skills.

assertions.assertion: Skill body has sufficient content density (>= 300 words) to be substantive, not a stub
assertions.rationale: A skill under 150 words is likely a stub that provides insufficient guidance for reliable execution.

assertions.assertion: YAML frontmatter parses without errors and contains no non-standard fields that would confuse validators
assertions.rationale: Invalid YAML breaks automated tooling. Non-standard fields (metadata, compatibility) are noise.
assertions.note: skippy-agentspace uses 'metadata' as a wrapper -- this is a known deviation from Anthropic spec that should be flattened

assertions.assertion: Skill name is kebab-case (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only), under 64 chars, contains no reserved words (anthropic, claude)
assertions.rationale: Naming convention compliance ensures skills work across all Claude Code installations and marketplaces.

assertions.assertion: Description is under 1024 characters, uses third-person voice (not 'I can' or 'You should'), and includes capability + trigger information
assertions.rationale: The description is injected into the system prompt's available_skills list. First/second person breaks the injection pattern.

assertions.assertion: No hardcoded absolute paths in SKILL.md body. All file references use relative paths, ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}, or {baseDir}
assertions.rationale: Absolute paths break portability. A skill installed on a different machine or in a different directory fails silently.

assertions.assertion: Body uses all applicable recommended sections: Overview, Prerequisites, Instructions, Output, Error Handling, Examples
assertions.rationale: Standard sections make skills predictable. Agents can reliably navigate to the section they need.

assertions.assertion: Instructions use imperative voice ('Analyze the diff', 'Run the tests') not passive or conditional ('The diff should be analyzed', 'You might want to run tests')
assertions.rationale: Imperative voice gives agents clear directives. Hedging language causes non-deterministic behavior.

assertions.assertion: Body avoids first person ('I can', 'I will', 'I help') and second person ('You can', 'You should') -- uses third person or imperative throughout
assertions.rationale: First/second person in skill bodies confuses the agent's sense of identity. The skill is instructions, not a conversation.

assertions.assertion: Body is concise -- under 2000 words, no unnecessary repetition, no filler paragraphs, no redundant explanations
assertions.rationale: Every word in SKILL.md costs tokens. Verbose skills eat into the context budget that should be used for actual work.

assertions.assertion: Skill name uses gerund form (verb-ing pattern, e.g., 'reviewing-prs', 'processing-data')
assertions.rationale: Gerund names are self-describing and improve grep discoverability per Anthropic naming recommendations.

assertions.assertion: SKILL.md has grep-friendly structure -- 7+ section headers with clear, searchable names
assertions.rationale: Grep-friendly structure means agents can find specific sections without reading the entire file.

assertions.assertion: Skill includes 3+ labeled, concrete examples with clear input/output pairs
assertions.rationale: Exemplary examples are the strongest signal for reliable agent execution. More examples = fewer misinterpretations.

assertions.assertion: Resources section includes links to external documentation or references/ files with real content
assertions.rationale: External links and reference files extend the skill without bloating the main body.

assertions.assertion: Skill uses dynamic context injection (!`file.md`) or ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} path references to load content on demand
assertions.rationale: Dynamic injection is the most sophisticated form of progressive disclosure -- content loaded only when the code path needs it.

assertions.assertion: Description uses first or second person ('I can', 'You should', 'I will help you')
assertions.rationale: Descriptions are injected into the system prompt's skill list. Wrong voice breaks the injection pattern and confuses routing.

assertions.assertion: Skill is a stub: < 30 lines body, < 150 words, no code blocks, no links, or > 5% placeholder tokens (TODO, FIXME, TBD)
assertions.rationale: Stubs waste a skill slot and context tokens without providing actionable guidance.

assertions.assertion: Body contains XML-like tags (<tags>, <output>) that are not inside code fences
assertions.rationale: XML tags outside code fences can be misinterpreted by the model as structural directives rather than content.

assertions.assertion: Body contains a Table of Contents section
assertions.rationale: TOCs waste tokens. Section headers are sufficient for navigation in a 150-line file.
